title: T-001 — organizations.onboarding_state JSONB column + Zod state schema
prompt:
Implement the persistent state contract for the 02-SETTINGS onboarding wizard described in PRD §14.3.

Goal: add a `onboarding_state` JSONB column to the `organizations` table and a typed Zod schema that the wizard server actions and UI will share.

Contract — JSONB shape per PRD §14.3 (line 1529):
  {
    "current_step": <int 1-6>,
    "completed_steps": <int[]>,
    "skipped_steps": <int[]>,
    "started_at": <ISO 8601 string>,
    "last_activity_at": <ISO 8601 string>
  }

Deliverable:
1. Drizzle migration adding `onboarding_state JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{"current_step":1,"completed_steps":[],"skipped_steps":[],"started_at":null,"last_activity_at":null}'::jsonb` (writable on insert by signup flow).
2. `packages/db/schema/organizations.ts` Drizzle column + inferred type.
3. `packages/shared/onboarding-state.schema.ts` Zod schema `onboardingStateSchema` exporting `OnboardingState` type.
4. pgTAP test: column exists, default applies on insert, JSONB structure matches schema.

Do NOT bundle: server actions (T-002), skip handler (T-003), or UI (T-004..T-010).

RED test command: `pnpm --filter db test -- onboarding_state`.

pipeline_inputs.description: Add organizations.onboarding_state JSONB column + Zod schema for the 6-step onboarding wizard state contract.
pipeline_inputs.details: Migration up adds the column with DEFAULT JSONB literal so existing rows backfill cleanly. Zod schema lives in shared package because both server actions (T-002, T-003) and wizard UI components (T-004..T-010) parse/serialize this shape. Inferred TS type re-exported from db schema barrel.
out_of_scope: Re-running existing migrations / changing other organizations columns
acceptance_criteria: Given an empty organizations row inserted, when SELECT onboarding_state runs, then JSONB matches default {current_step:1, completed_steps:[], skipped_steps:[], started_at:null, last_activity_at:null}.
Given onboardingStateSchema imported in any package, when invalid shape (e.g., current_step:0 or 7) is parsed, then Zod throws with field-level error.
Given pgTAP test runs, when migration is applied to clean DB, then test asserts column exists with correct DEFAULT and NOT NULL constraint and passes 0 failures.
Given pnpm --filter db test runs, when this migration is in the chain, then the suite is green and no other migrations are touched.
test_strategy: RED: write pgTAP test asserting column + DEFAULT BEFORE writing migration; confirm RED (column missing).
GREEN: apply migration, re-run, all 4 AC pass.
risk_red_lines: Do not change other columns of organizations table.
Do not skip the DEFAULT clause — existing rows must backfill.
Do not place the Zod schema inside apps/web (must be in packages/shared so both server actions and UI consume it).
